description: Coding schema for AI interviewer turn-level analysis. Edit this file to adapt the toolkit to your dataset.

AI.opening: First turn of the interview; establishes context and introduces the study
AI.new_question: Moves to a new topic, whether as a cold shift or wrapped in praise/acknowledgment of the previous answer
AI.follow_up: Asks for elaboration, clarification, or deeper exploration of the participant's immediately preceding response
AI.closing: Final turn(s) of the interview; wraps up and thanks the participant
AI.ambiguous: Could plausibly be classified as multiple types; requires review
AI.other: Does not fit any of the above categories; add a note describing why

User.response: Participant responds to the AI's question or statement
User.other: Does not fit the response category; add a note

biasing_response_note: Auto-detection only catches explicit praise patterns. Affirmative reframes, leading interpretations, and vocabulary substitution require manual flagging during coding.

opening.note: Auto-labeled by position. Add match_pattern entries below to catch recurring opening questions that appear after the first turn.

closing.note: Auto-labeled by position. Add match_pattern entries below to catch standard closing phrases that appear before the final turn.

response.note: Auto-labeled by speaker. Edit individual turns if needed.

model_note: Model used for LLM classification. claude-sonnet-4-6 is the default (fast, cost-effective). Switch to claude-opus-4-8 if kappa scores are poor and you want stronger performance on nuanced judgment calls — it costs more per transcript but handles ambiguous cases better.

sample.note: Change n to set the total number of transcripts to sample. Change seed to get a different random draw (set to null for a random seed). To sample a fixed number per group, add a 'splits' object: {"group_a": 20, "group_b": 20}. The split names must match values in the 'split' column of your transcripts table.
